data.title: After the ICO failed and TROVE plummeted by 99%, TroveMarkets secretly refunded KOLs via stablecoins.
data.description: According to Bubblemaps data, after Trove's $11.5 million ICO failed and TROVE's price dropped by 99%, it secretly refunded KOLs via stablecoin transfers, while pre-sale participants faced losses.

data.title: A whale closed its short positions in HYPE and XMR, making a profit of approximately $3.1 million.
data.description: According to The Data Nerd, 12 hours ago, a whale (0x880a...311c) began closing out its short positions in HYPE and XMR. The two trades are expected to generate a profit of approximately $3.1 million.

data.title: Linea Team Transfers $5.3 Million in Tokens to New Wallet
data.description: The Linea team has transferred $5.3 million worth of LINEA tokens to a new wallet, according to ChainCatcher. This transaction occurred approximately 10 minutes ago. Historical data suggests that similar transfers are often followed by a gradual movement of funds to exchanges.

data.title: Trump's Deportation Efforts Lead to Increased Adoption of Sanctuary Laws
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has consistently opposed 'sanctuary' laws throughout his tenure. Bloomberg posted on X that as his extensive deportation initiatives create turmoil across American streets, an increasing number of states and cities governed by Democrats are implementing these laws. This shift reflects a growing resistance to federal immigration policies under Trump's administration.

data.title: AI Agent Payments: The Role of Cryptocurrency
data.description: Crypto KOL Xingpt posted on X. A discussion has emerged regarding whether payments between AI agents necessarily require cryptocurrency. While it is not mandatory to use crypto for such transactions, the inherent structure of native payments between AI agents tends to favor the use of cryptocurrency. Traditional payment systems like Visa and Mastercard are also options, but the decentralized nature of crypto offers distinct advantages in facilitating these transactions.

data.title: Schroders' Potential Growth May Benefit Areas Beyond London
data.description: Schroders, a prominent asset management company, is poised for growth, but the benefits may extend beyond the City of London. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that while Schroders has substantial financial backing, the positive impacts of its expansion are likely to be felt in regions outside of London's financial district. This development suggests a shift in the distribution of economic benefits, potentially influencing other areas. The company's strategic moves could lead to broader economic implications, affecting various stakeholders in the financial sector.

data.title: U.S. Stock Market Opens with Gains as Energy Stocks Rise
data.description: The U.S. stock market opened with modest gains, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average increasing by 0.1%, the S&P 500 rising by 0.3%, and the Nasdaq Composite climbing by 0.38%. According to Jin10, storage-related stocks showed strength, with Micron Technology gaining 3.1% and SanDisk rising by 6.2%. Peabody Energy saw a 2.5% increase following U.S. President Donald Trump's directive for the Department of Energy to allocate funds to maintain coal-fired power plant operations.

data.title: US stocks opened mixed in the crypto sector, with MSTR rising 1.6%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the opening of the US stock market, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.1%, the S&P 500 rose 0.3%, and the Nasdaq Composite rose 0.38%. Cryptocurrency stocks showed mixed performance: MSTR rose 1.6%, CRCL fell 0.36%, SBET rose 1.51%, BMNR rose 1.13%, and COIN fell 1.01%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: AI Adoption Widespread, but Profit Gains Limited, Says McKinsey
data.description: Almost all companies are experimenting with artificial intelligence, according to McKinsey's Greater China chairman. However, only 5% of these firms believe that AI is enhancing their profits. According to NS3.AI, the main challenge is that companies are not restructuring their organizations adequately to fully leverage AI's potential for profit. This indicates a broad adoption of AI technology without significant strategic transformation.

data.title: IMF Highlights Challenges Facing German Economy
data.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has reported that the German economy has faced significant challenges in recent years. According to Jin10, these difficulties have been exacerbated by sluggish growth in fundamental productivity. The IMF's assessment underscores the ongoing economic pressures Germany is experiencing, which could have broader implications for the European economy.

data.title: ESP (Espresso) rose above 0.08 USDT
data.description: Market data shows that ESP (Espresso) has risen above 0.08 USDT and is currently trading at 0.085 USDT.

data.title: BlackRock deposited 1,134 BTC, worth $77.05 million, into Coinbase.
data.description: According to The Data Nerd, BlackRock deposited 1,134 BTC, worth $77.05 million, into Coinbase two hours ago.

data.title: Ondo Finance Launches Tokenized Stocks on DeFi Lending Market
data.description: Ondo Finance has announced the launch of tokenized stocks from Ondo Global Markets on the DeFi lending market. According to Foresight News, the initial offerings include SPYon and QQQon. Through integration with Morpho and risk management by Gauntlet, Ondo's tokenized U.S. stocks and ETFs can now serve as efficiently managed collateral assets within Ethereum's DeFi ecosystem.

data.title: White House Adviser Criticizes North American Trade Pact Ahead of Negotiations
data.description: White House trade adviser Peter Navarro has expressed concerns about the North American trade pact, highlighting what he describes as 'significant flaws' in the agreement. Bloomberg posted on X, noting that these comments come as negotiations over the deal are set to begin. Navarro's remarks underscore the administration's intent to address issues within the trade agreement, which has been a point of contention in U.S. trade policy. The adviser emphasized the need for revisions to ensure fair trade practices and economic benefits for all parties involved. As discussions progress, the focus will be on resolving these identified shortcomings to strengthen the trade relationship between the countries.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Proposes Ethereum as Settlement Layer for AI API Usage
data.description: Vitalik Buterin, co-founder of Ethereum, has proposed utilizing Ethereum as a settlement layer for privacy-preserving metered AI API usage. According to NS3.AI, this approach would not involve running AI models on-chain but would instead employ zero-knowledge proofs and deposit-based credits. This system aims to enable users to pay for large volumes of API calls without revealing their identities, addressing the increasing enterprise adoption of AI agents. However, a significant challenge lies in metadata correlation, which could compromise privacy. Additionally, adoption of this system requires overcoming technical, operational, and coordination hurdles.

data.title: Yen Expected to Strengthen as Japan's Fiscal Policies Shift
data.description: Rabobank strategist Jane Foley highlights that the recent election victory of Japan's Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi supports a return to responsible fiscal policies. According to NS3.AI, this political shift, combined with anticipated interest rate hikes by the Bank of Japan, is expected to significantly strengthen the yen. Consequently, the USD/JPY exchange rate is forecasted to decline from approximately 153 to 145 within the next year.

data.title: Mercuria Ends Decade-Long Chinese State-Linked Ownership
data.description: Mercuria has repurchased its shares from CNIC, concluding a ten-year period of minority ownership by entities associated with the Chinese state. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this significant move in the commodity trading sector. This buyback marks a strategic shift for Mercuria, one of the largest commodity traders globally, as it regains full control over its shares previously held by Chinese state-linked companies. The decision underscores Mercuria's intent to consolidate its ownership structure and potentially streamline its operations moving forward.

data.title: La Niña's Influence on Global Weather Expected to Diminish by April
data.description: The La Niña weather pattern, which has been impacting global weather since last year, is anticipated to weaken in the coming months. Bloomberg posted on X that the Pacific Ocean is expected to return to a neutral state by April. This shift could have significant implications for weather patterns worldwide, potentially affecting agriculture, water resources, and disaster preparedness. The transition to a neutral state may bring changes in temperature and precipitation patterns, influencing various sectors dependent on climate conditions.

data.title: Market news: An internal Russian memo mentions a return to the dollar system, aimed at gaining Trump's support.
data.description: Market news: An internal Russian memo mentions a return to the dollar system, intended to garner Trump's support. It also mentions joint plans in the energy, mining, and fossil fuel sectors. (Jinshi)

data.title: Gradient releases Echo-2 RL framework to improve AI research efficiency.
data.description: Gradient, a distributed AI lab, today released Echo-2, a distributed reinforcement learning framework designed to break down training efficiency barriers in AI research. By completely decoupling Learner and Actor at the architectural level, Echo-2 drastically reduces the post-training cost of a 30-bit model from $4,500 to $425. This translates to over 10 times the research throughput within the same budget. The framework utilizes a storage-computation separation technique for asynchronous training (Async RL), offloading massive sampling computations to unstable GPU instances and heterogeneous GPUs based on Parallax. Combined with breakthroughs in bounded stagnation, fault-tolerant instance scheduling, and the proprietary Lattica communication protocol, it significantly improves training efficiency while maintaining model accuracy. Alongside the framework release, Gradient will also soon launch its RLaaS platform, Logits, driving a shift in AI research from a "capital-driven" to an "efficiency-based" paradigm. Logits is now open for reservations by students and researchers worldwide (logits.dev). Gradient is an AI lab dedicated to building distributed infrastructure, focusing on the distributed training, service, and deployment of cutting-edge large models.
